meta.title: Yardi Matrix — National Multifamily Report
meta.methodology: Yardi Matrix tracks ~17 million multifamily units across the US. The free monthly Multifamily Report covers asking-rent growth (YoY + MoM) and occupancy rate by metro for the top 30+ markets. Sample is biased toward stabilized class-A/B properties, so growth rates skew slightly slower than CoStar's or RealPage's. Yardi is widely cited by trade press (Bisnow, Multifamily Dive, NMHC) — using it gives a consistent benchmark against industry coverage.
meta.important_disclaimer: Numbers below are COHO's best-effort directional estimates from publicly-available Yardi Matrix coverage. For IC memos, ALWAYS pull the latest Yardi Matrix monthly report directly. Yardi publishes data primarily on stabilized properties, which lags new-product trends; cross-check with Apartment List Rent Index (also free) and Zillow ZORI (in COHO's Economic Dashboard).

national.narrative: National asking-rent growth has decelerated from 8%+ (2021-22) to ~1.4% YoY as new-construction deliveries from the 2021-2022 capital-stack rush peak in 2024-2026. Occupancy stable at 94.7%. Most metros now seeing modest single-digit growth; sun-belt metros that overbuilt are flat to negative YoY.

denver_msa.notes: Denver MSA absorbed a record 12k+ deliveries in 2024, pulling rent growth flat. Q2 2026 deliveries declining. Expected to return to 2-3% YoY by mid-2026 as new supply pipeline thins.

colorado_springs_msa.notes: Springs metro tracks slightly above national avg — population growth + smaller delivery pipeline.

fort_collins_msa.notes: Fort Collins / Loveland — CSU economy keeps demand stable.

boulder_msa.notes: Highest rent + tightest occupancy of CO metros. Constrained supply + research-economy demand.

lihtc_dev_implications.what_this_says_for_underwriting: Yardi data is the bridge between LIHTC's restricted rent + market rent. For 80% AMI tiers, the gap between LIHTC ceiling and market rent (Yardi median) is the 'rent capture advantage' — how much headroom your project has. For 60% AMI tiers, the gap is usually meaningful in any growing metro.
lihtc_dev_implications.rent_growth_assumption: Use 2-3% annual rent growth for 30-yr pro forma in Denver MSA (matches Yardi national + CO-specific deceleration). Conservative for resort markets (use 3-4%); aggressive for rural (use 1.5-2%).
lihtc_dev_implications.cross_check: Yardi numbers should be within 5-10% of Apartment List rent index (similar methodology, different sample). Big divergence usually signals one source has stale or noisy data.
